meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Faster: Advanced User Onboarding for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with cryptic bugs. Learn to leverage user onboarding flows with Antigravity and Claude Code to isolate issues and ship production-ready apps.
content.answer_block: Debugging is often a symptom of poor state management during user onboarding. By instrumenting your onboarding flow with telemetry and using Claude Code to analyze the execution trace, you can isolate failures in real-time. This structural approach transforms vague errors into actionable, fixable code modules for your clients.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank terminal, the client’s deadline is looming, and a ghost in the machine is crashing the onboarding flow. You’ve refactored the logic three times, yet the error persists. It’s not just a bug; it’s a bottleneck that threatens your reputation and your bottom line.

True senior-level velocity isn't about writing code faster; it's about building systems that explain themselves. By turning your user onboarding into a diagnostic engine, you stop guessing and start engineering. We’re going to swap the tedious manual debugging cycle for an automated, AI-driven observability pipeline.

steps.title: Architect the Telemetry Layer
steps.description: Inject event listeners into every onboarding step to capture state snapshots before failure occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current onboarding component to emit structured JSON event logs at every state transition. Include user context, step ID, and current local state.

steps.title: Define the Failure Boundary
steps.description: Create a wrapper that catches and contextualizes errors specifically for the onboarding journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-order function that wraps all onboarding async calls, capturing errors with stack traces and current input parameters.
steps.claude_code_command: claude-code --run 'generate error boundary wrapper for onboarding'

steps.title: Simulate Edge-Case Onboarding
steps.description: Use AI to generate synthetic user data that triggers potential race conditions during the sign-up phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of test cases simulating fragmented network speeds and incomplete user data during the multi-step onboarding process.

steps.title: Analyze Trace Logs with Claude
steps.description: Feed the captured telemetry into Claude Code to identify the exact point of execution failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided log files from the onboarding session and identify the root cause of the intermittent state reset.

steps.title: Automated Remediation
steps.description: Apply the fix generated by the analysis directly into the codebase with automated verification.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a code patch to fix the identified race condition in the onboarding state manager and include a regression test.

pro_tips: Always log the 'previous state' alongside the 'new state' to identify state-mutation bugs instantly.
Use Claude Code to generate documentation for your onboarding flow as you build; it forces cleaner code structure.
Implement a 'dry run' mode in your onboarding logic that logs everything to a local file instead of production DBs.
Treat your onboarding telemetry as a permanent product feature, not just a debugging tool for launch.
Use Antigravity to generate visual flowcharts from your code to spot logic gaps before you even start typing.
content.next_steps: Integrate your new onboarding telemetry into a real-time dashboard to monitor client sign-up success rates.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own code and start shipping features that delight your clients. Master the workflow that separates the top 1% of AI-powered freelancers from the rest. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
